incidents.vector: Armenian national extradited to US for allegedly developing and administering the RedLine infostealer malware, distributing it to affiliates via file-sharing platforms.
incidents.impact: RedLine is one of the most prevalent infostealer variants globally, responsible for stealing billions of credentials. Hambardzum Minasyan charged with conspiracy to commit access device fraud and computer fraud. Follows 2024 Operation Magnus disruption of RedLine and Meta infostealers.

incidents.vector: Attacker compromised a private key used to authorize stablecoin minting, bypassing on-chain controls that lacked a maximum mint limit.
incidents.impact: Attacker minted $80 million in uncollateralized USR stablecoin and traded it for approximately $24.5 million in ETH. USR depegged from the US dollar and dropped to $0.26. Platform paused operations and is pursuing recovery.

incidents.victim: US companies targeted by Yanluowang ransomware
incidents.vector: Russian national Aleksei Volkov operated as an initial access broker, exploiting corporate network vulnerabilities and selling access to the Yanluowang ransomware group.
incidents.impact: Volkov sentenced to 81 months in federal prison. His brokerage facilitated dozens of attacks causing over $9 million in confirmed losses and $24 million in intended losses across seven identified US businesses. Ordered to pay $9.1 million in restitution.

incidents.victim: Iranian organizations and cloud infrastructure users
incidents.vector: TeamPCP deployed the CanisterWorm wiper through compromised cloud infrastructure, using blockchain-based ICP canisters for command and control. The wiper activates when it detects Iran's timezone or Farsi locale settings.
incidents.impact: Systems matching Iranian locale have data destroyed; if the victim has Kubernetes access, data is wiped across all cluster nodes. Leveraged same infrastructure as the Trivy supply chain attack. Actual impact on Iranian systems is unclear.

incidents.victim: Aqua Security (Trivy), Checkmarx (KICS), liteLLM, and downstream users
incidents.vector: TeamPCP used a stolen privileged personal access token from Aqua Security to inject credential-stealing malware into Trivy vulnerability scanner releases on GitHub. They then replaced existing binary versions (not new releases) to evade version-pinning defenses. The compromise cascaded to Checkmarx KICS and liteLLM.
incidents.impact: Malware exfiltrated SSH keys, cloud credentials, Kubernetes tokens, and cryptocurrency wallets from users running compromised versions. liteLLM proxy compromise exposed LLM API credentials. Aqua Security has remediated multiple times but attackers retained some access. Potentially affects thousands of development environments.

incidents.vector: Four botnets (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad) compromised over 3 million IoT devices including routers and web cameras, using them to launch DDoS attacks including against US Department of Defense addresses.
incidents.impact: DOJ, Canada, and Germany dismantled the botnet infrastructure. Over 200,000 attack commands issued by Aisuru alone. Victims reported tens of thousands of dollars in losses. Operators demanded extortion payments from DDoS targets.

incidents.victim: Tycoon2FA platform targets (Microsoft, Google, Apple account holders)
incidents.vector: The Tycoon2FA phishing-as-a-service platform creates convincing pages that impersonate legitimate 2FA authentication flows for Microsoft, Google, and Apple accounts, intercepting session tokens to bypass MFA.
incidents.impact: Platform continues to operate despite law enforcement takedown attempts. Operators migrated to bulletproof hosting with enhanced anti-forensics and evasion techniques. Targets finance, healthcare, and technology sectors globally.

incidents.vector: Iran conducted cyber-enabled psychological operations targeting US audiences during the ongoing military conflict.
incidents.impact: DOJ disrupted the Iranian cyber-enabled psychological operations. Details of the scope and methods remain limited, but action was taken amid heightened Iran-US tensions.

incidents.vector: Three individuals conspired to unlawfully divert cutting-edge US artificial intelligence technology to China, circumventing export controls.
incidents.impact: DOJ filed charges against three defendants in the Southern District of New York. The case reflects ongoing concerns about Chinese efforts to acquire advanced AI capabilities through illicit means.

incidents.victim: Thousands of US persons and businesses
incidents.vector: Operators ran a global malicious proxy service that deployed malware to conscript victim devices into a proxy network, then sold access for use in fraud and other cybercrime.
incidents.impact: International authorities (US, Canada, Germany) dismantled the proxy infrastructure. The service defrauded thousands of US persons, businesses, and financial institutions, causing millions of dollars in losses.

incidents.vector: Iran-backed Handala group compromised administrative credentials and abused Microsoft Intune endpoint management to push remote wipe commands across Stryker's global Microsoft environment.
incidents.impact: Over 200,000 devices wiped across offices in 79 countries. 5,000+ workers sent home in Ireland alone. Systems, employee devices (including personal phones with Outlook), and login pages defaced with the Handala logo. CISA issued an alert urging endpoint management hardening. Claimed as retaliation for a deadly missile strike on an Iranian school.

incidents.vector: Iran-backed Handala group gained access to corporate networks using stolen legitimate user credentials, then deployed wipers to delete servers and workstations across targeted Israeli organizations.
incidents.impact: Israel's National Cyber Directorate issued a warning on March 6. Multiple organizations affected with servers and workstations deleted. Part of a broader Iranian cyber campaign alongside the ongoing military conflict. Unit 42 assessed increased risk of wiper attacks related to the Iran conflict.

incidents.vector: Attackers used custom backdoors (AppleChris, MemFun) and a credential harvester (Getpass), spreading laterally via WMI and Windows .NET commands. Persistence achieved through DLL hijacking of the Volume Shadow Copy Service and new service creation. Dead drop resolver technique used Pastebin for C2 resolution.
incidents.impact: Attackers selectively searched for and exfiltrated sensitive military files including official meeting records, joint military activities with Western forces, and C4I system assessments. Campaign traced back to 2020 with periods of deliberate dormancy. Domain controllers, web servers, and executive-level assets were targeted.

incidents.victim: UAE energy and marine services company (and regional diplomatic targets)
incidents.vector: Iran's Boggy Serpens group conducted four distinct attack waves over six months using highly tailored spear phishing lures (engineering reports, financial documents, fake airline tickets), deploying custom backdoors including BlackBeard, GhostBackDoor, and Nuso via hijacked legitimate email accounts.
incidents.impact: Sustained cyberespionage campaign targeting a strategic UAE energy entity with ties to Saudi Aramco. The group also targeted entities in Israel, Hungary, Turkey, Saudi Arabia, Egypt, and South America. AI-enhanced malware with anti-analysis techniques used for long-term persistence. Represents a significant escalation in Iranian cyber capabilities.

incidents.vector: Pay2Key ransomware operators compromised an administrative account days before deploying ransomware, then encrypted the environment and cleared event logs to cover their tracks.
incidents.impact: Healthcare organization's environment encrypted. No evidence of data exfiltration. Attack occurred concurrently with US-Iran military conflict. Pay2Key has targeted 170 victims and collected $8 million in ransoms since summer 2025. Researchers noted motivations "extend well beyond typical financially driven ransomware operations."

incidents.victim: AWS cloud customers (Bahrain and UAE regions)
incidents.vector: Drone activity near AWS data center facilities in Bahrain disrupted cloud operations. This is the second drone-related disruption to AWS Middle East regions in a month amid the US-Israel-Iran conflict.
incidents.impact: AWS Bahrain region taken offline. Earlier in March, structural damage, power loss, and water damage from fire suppression hit both Bahrain and UAE AWS facilities. Amazon is migrating customers to alternate regions but recovery timeline remains unclear. Major impact on organizations relying on Middle East cloud presence.
